name: lavatree
comment: DEPRECATED - implicitly superseded by the Iris 4.0 cave-carving method. This baked obsidian-encased lava-object was a workaround to fake a carved lava pocket/volcano under Iris 3.9; the Iris 4.0 noise-field carver (a surface-breaking lava conduit via caveProfile + allowLava + a dimension carving[] band, see docs/design/IRIS-V4-CARVING.md) now produces real carved lava terrain, so this object hack is no longer the intended approach. Kept in-repo unreferenced for history/reference only; do not wire into new biomes. Inverted obsidian-encased lava tree for the Frostpeak Caldera. A wide ROUND lava trunk with many lava branches; the whole lava mass (trunk + branches) is fully sheathed in obsidian by the 'encase' post-process (every exposed lava face -> obsidian), so the trunk reads as a rounded obsidian-skinned column with a lava core. invert_y flips it so it drives DOWN into the ground as a buried lava root-mass. Placed very densely so neighbouring trunks overlap and their obsidian sheaths merge into irregular surface crusts. (Fire roots dropped: Iris does not tick placed fire so it just floats.)

name: lavatreebig
comment: DEPRECATED - implicitly superseded by the Iris 4.0 cave-carving method (see docs/design/IRIS-V4-CARVING.md and the 'lavatree' entry above). Kept in-repo unreferenced for history/reference only; do not wire into new biomes. Large summit centerpiece variant of the lava tree (wide ~32 trunk). One placed per Frostpeak summit at 100% chance. Same obsidian-encased, round, inverted, void_air-root design as the small lavatree.
